narrative:
Short impact summary

The TNO Image Fusion Dataset (2014), hosted on Figshare, has been cited 202 times, demonstrating its significance within the fields of imaging and data fusion. With 206 instances of subsequent research relying on its data, it showcases the dataset's substantial role as a foundational research output.

Full narrative

The TNO Image Fusion Dataset (2014) represents an essential dataset within the research community, having garnered 202 citations across various scholarly works. This impactful resource has not only facilitated inquiries in image processing but has also sparked the reuse of its data in 207 subsequent research projects, highlighting its enduring applicability.

The dataset has seen notable uptake in several key academic venues, with citations appearing in leading journals such as Infrared Physics & Technology and Information Fusion. Such recognition underscores the dataset's relevance in advancing knowledge and applications related to imaging technologies.

Despite a lack of online mentions in social and community discourse platforms, the dataset's utility in the academic domain cannot be understated. It serves as a reliable reference point for researchers pursuing investigations that leverage image fusion methodologies, further evidenced by its substantial view (106) and download (37) counts on Figshare.

The absence of funding agencies supporting this dataset does not undermine its credibility; rather, it positions the dataset as a product of dedicated research efforts within the community. Enabling access to high-quality data, the TNO Image Fusion Dataset stands as a testament to the potential for datasets to drive research innovation and development in computational imaging and related fields.
